company_name: Resonac Holdings Corporation (formerly Showa Denko K.K.)
founded: 1939 (as Showa Denko); Resonac Holdings formed January 1, 2023
abf_relation: Key supplier of semiconductor packaging materials including ABF substrate precursor materials. Formed through merger of Showa Denko K.K. and Showa Denko Materials Co., Ltd. (former Hitachi Chemical Co., Ltd.). Hitachi Chemical was a major ABF substrate material supplier.

semiconductor_materials: ABF substrate materials (via former Hitachi Chemical)

strengths: Vertically integrated semiconductor materials supplier (front-end + back-end)
Inherited Hitachi Chemical's ABF substrate material capabilities
Diversified chemical portfolio reduces single-market risk
Strong position in SiC and advanced packaging materials
Consolidated revenue of ~$9B demonstrates significant scale
weaknesses: Recent merger integration may create operational complexity
opportunities: Growing ABF substrate demand driven by AI/HPC chip packaging
SiC materials for EV power semiconductors
Consolidation benefits from Showa Denko + Hitachi Chemical merger

review_notes: Resonac (Showa Denko + Hitachi Chemical merger) is a confirmed key player in semiconductor materials including ABF substrate precursors. Hitachi Chemical was already reviewed (score 62). Resonac provides the parent company perspective and consolidated financials.
